text.title: pytest Configuration
text.summary: pyproject.toml setup, essential plugins, markers, coverage with pytest-cov, parallel execution with xdist, and asyncio mode.

section.title: Essential Plugins
section.rule: Pin plugin versions in requirements-dev.txt or pyproject.toml dev dependencies.

example.code:
[tool.pytest.ini_options] testpaths = ["tests"] python_files = ["test_*.py"] python_classes = ["Test*"] python_functions = ["test_*"] addopts = [ "--strict-markers", "--tb=short", "--cov=src", "--cov-report=term-missing", "--cov-report=html", "--cov-fail-under=80", ] asyncio_mode = "auto" markers = [ "slow: marks tests as slow (deselect with '-m \"not slow\"')", "integration: marks integration tests requiring external services", "e2e: marks end-to-end tests", ]
[tool.coverage.run] source = ["src"] omit = ["src/migrations/*", "src/settings/*"]
[tool.coverage.report] exclude_lines = [ "pragma: no cover", "if TYPE_CHECKING:", "raise NotImplementedError", ]

section.title: Markers
section.rule: Declare all markers in pyproject.toml with --strict-markers to prevent typos.
Use markers to categorize slow or integration tests and skip them in fast feedback loops.

example.code:
# Fast unit tests only pytest -m "not slow and not integration"
# All including slow pytest
# Integration only pytest -m integration

section.title: Parallel Execution
section.rule: Use pytest-xdist with -n auto for CPU-bound test suites; be aware of fixture scope issues.
Session-scoped fixtures are NOT shared across xdist workers — use module or function scope, or a tmp_path-based workaround.

antipattern.description: Session-scoped DB fixture breaks under xdist
antipattern.reason: Each worker gets its own session — database sequences can collide. Use separate DB per worker with worker_id fixture.
